accessibilityFixes.fix: Added aria-label='Play video' to play button in both TSX and PHP
Added aria-hidden='true' to IconPlay SVG in both TSX and PHP
Added title='YouTube video player' / 'Vimeo video player' to all iframes in TSX playVideo(), PHP static render, and frontend JS

minorFixes.issue: Dark mode border radius inconsistency - single image layout used rounded-3xl while gallery used rounded-xl
minorFixes.fix: Normalized single image layout to use rounded-xl in dark mode, matching gallery behavior

warnings: Pre-existing warnings about experimental WordPress components (__experimentalToggleGroupControl, etc.) - not introduced by this change
Pre-existing React Hook useEffect warning about mediaRenderId dependency - not introduced by this change
Asset size warnings are normal for this project

syncStatus.notes: TSX and PHP changes kept in sync. All aria-label, aria-hidden, and title attributes added to both editor and frontend rendering.

implementedFixes.description: YouTube/Vimeo iframes have no title attribute
implementedFixes.location: ContentWithMedia.tsx lines 132, 140; ContentWithMedia.php line 238; ContentWithMedia.js lines 30, 32

implementedFixes.description: Single image layout border radius inconsistency in dark mode

notImplemented.description: headingSize attribute not added - requires design decision
notImplemented.reason: The planning document identified this as a potential addition from block-type spec but noted it as 'minor' severity. The block currently hardcodes headingSize=2 (H2). Adding the attribute would require coordination with other blocks using ThemeHeading and isn't strictly an accessibility fix. Recommend adding in a separate enhancement task.

notes: All accessibility fixes from planning-2026-02-12-204714.json have been implemented
TSX and PHP remain perfectly synchronized with identical aria-label and title attributes
Build passes with no new errors introduced by these changes
Test page successfully created with all 14 scenarios using DemoContainer pattern
Block count verification confirms all 14 instances were created correctly
The headingSize attribute (GAP-004) was intentionally not added as it requires design/UX decision and is not strictly an accessibility requirement
